metadata.description: Static analysis heatmap detecting four categories of legacy patterns in eduagents3.0 pipeline

summary.keyInsight: Legacy item field mappings (prompt/answer/options/passage) are widely used during orchestration and building phases. Direct slot mutations in Architect warrant consolidation.

categoryA_legacyWriterFields.description: Writer metadata fields appearing OUTSIDE metadata context (difficulty, cognitiveDemand, questionType, templateId, etc.)

locations.issue: Slots are mutated directly rather than through builder/normalization pattern
locations.recommendation: Use immutable update pattern or wrap in metadata builder

locations.pattern: Direct field assignment in compensation object
locations.code: compensation.difficulty = `Match ${domain} difficulty exactly to the blueprint.`;
locations.issue: Assigning string description to difficulty field that should be enum
locations.recommendation: Use separate description field or rename to compensationReason
locations.risk: Type mismatch; difficulty field should be 'easy'|'medium'|'hard', not string message

categoryB_legacyInferenceLogic.description: Legacy inference functions (guess, infer, fallback, defaultDifficulty, determineQuestionType, etc.)
categoryB_legacyInferenceLogic.note: No legacy inference functions detected. 'fallback' references are all documented Writer fallback routing (legitimate).

categoryC_legacyBlueprintCoupling.description: References to blueprint.* outside Orchestrator (indicates tight coupling to architecture plan)
categoryC_legacyBlueprintCoupling.note: No blueprint. references detected in Writer, Gatekeeper, Builder, or Scribe agents. Blueprint coupling properly isolated to Orchestrator.

categoryD_legacyItemShapes.description: Items using prompt|answer|options|passage fields directly without metadata wrapper context

locations.issue: Handles legacy problemText/correctAnswer fields by mapping to prompt/answer
locations.recommendation: Consolidate schema to single naming; add legacy migration layer if needed
locations.risk: Creates dual-schema state; users can interact with either old or new naming

locations.pattern: Legacy field mapping during build phase
locations.recommendation: Extract to shared normalization utility; apply once at entry
locations.risk: Code duplication; builder repeats orchestrator's schema handling
locations.frequency: High: builder is called for all rendered items

locations.pattern: Reading item.prompt directly without metadata extraction
locations.issue: Accesses prompt field directly for text analysis
locations.recommendation: Wrap in metadata accessor: item.metadata?.prompt ?? item.prompt as fallback
locations.risk: Tight coupling to item shape; hard to refactor to metadata-only model

locations.issue: Mutates item.options directly outside structured builder
locations.recommendation: Consolidate to builder pattern or metadata normalization
locations.risk: Bypasses validation; side-effects on item object

locations.issue: Accesses prompt directly without metadata context
locations.risk: Assumes item.prompt exists; may miss metadata-only items

locations.pattern: Reading passage and prompt fields directly
locations.issue: Direct field access for validation logic
locations.recommendation: Extract to item accessor that respects metadata structure
locations.risk: Assumes flat item shape; breaks if schema becomes hierarchical

locations.issue: Checks prompt field without metadata wrapper awareness
locations.risk: Format detection may fail if prompt moved to metadata

locations.pattern: Extracting all four legacy fields for output
locations.issue: Final exporter reads flat schema; assumes prompt/answer/options/passage
locations.recommendation: Map from metadata structure to output schema
locations.risk: Metadata-only items will output empty prompt/answer

refactoringPriority.reason: CRITICAL: Final exporter must handle both flat and metadata schemas
HIGH: Validation logic runs on all items; impacts system stability
MEDIUM: Duplicate field mapping logic; consolidate to single normalization
MEDIUM: Direct slot mutations bypass validation
LOW: Type mismatch; fix field semantics

writer.note: Writer agent has no blueprint.* references; properly isolated

orchestrator.note: Properly owns blueprint routing, but duplicates field normalization with Builder

pluginEngine.note: Generates items with metadata structure; few direct field accesses

nextSteps: 1. Create `src/pipeline/utils/itemNormalizer.ts` - single-source-of-truth for legacy → modern schema mapping
2. Refactor Orchestrator and Builder to both use itemNormalizer
3. Add item accessor utility: `getPrompt(item)`, `getAnswer(item)`, `getOptions(item)` that respect metadata
4. Update Scribe exporter to use accessors
5. Update Gatekeeper validation to use accessors
6. Remove direct slot mutation in adjustPlanForTime; use immutable builders
7. Run full integration tests on dry-run suite to validate
